The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

revision.comment: 107 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:津島神社 up to rev 101876956
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|愛知県津島市にある津島神社|その他の津島神社|津島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{神社 | 名称=津島神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tsushima-jinja_Shrine_Haiden_(1),_Shimmei-cho_Tsushima_City_2023.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿
（2023年（令和5年）4月）
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|10|41.6|N|136|43|7.1|E}}}} | 所在地=[[愛知県]][[津島市]]神明町1 | ISO = JP-23 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 10|緯度秒 = 41.65 | 経度度 =136|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 7.13 | 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] | 創建=[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]） | 社格=旧[[国幣小社]]
[[別表神社]] | 本殿=[[尾張造]] | 例祭=[[6月15日]] | 神事=津島天王祭（7月第4土曜日） | 地図=Japan Aichi }} '''津島神社'''（つしまじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[神社]]。京都・[[八坂神社]]とともに牛頭天王信仰の二大社として知られる{{Cite web|和書|title=第四章 津島神社の「花の撓」|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/minzoku/mukei_kiroku/pdf/r1392266_08.pdf |website=文化庁 |access-date=2023-12-30}}。 == 概要 == [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]包括の[[別表神社]]。 [[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、大穴牟遅命（[[大国主]]）を相殿に祀る。伝承では[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]）の創建とされる{{Cite web|和書|title=第2章 維持及び向上すべき歴史的風致|url=https://www.city.tsushima.lg.jp/shisei/machidukuri/rekisitekihuuti/tsushimashirekimachi.files/2syo.pdf |website=津島市 |access-date=2023-12-30}}。実際の創建時期ははっきりしないが、12世紀の文献に現れ、鎌倉時代から室町時代にかけての鉄灯籠や梵鐘の銘には社名が刻まれている。近世以前は[[牛頭天王]]を祭神として祀る神社として知られた{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.14.0_15 |author=松下 孜 |title=天王信仰と津島御師の活動 御立符と檀那場をめぐって |journal=愛知県史研究 |volume=14 |issue= |publisher=愛知県 |year=2010 |pages= 15-37}}。 近世には織田氏や豊臣氏の庇護を受けて社殿造営等の寄進を受けた。さらに尾張藩からも神領所有の特権を得るとともに、津島神社の川祭りで寄進米などを受ける特権が与えられた。 当社は[[東海地方]]を中心に全国に約3千社ある津島神社・天王社の総本社であり、その信仰を[[津島信仰]]という。津島神社が諸国に勢力を拡大した背景には、御札を配って祈祷を行う津島御師の活動が大きく、織田信長は御師の布教活動を保護し、尾張藩もこの保護政策を継承して他領での布教活動に許可を与えた。 明治の[[神仏分離令]]までは「津島牛頭天王社」とも称された。 == 歴史 == [[File:Old tenno river map.jpg|thumb|250px|right|江戸時代末ごろの[[天王川 (津島市)|天王川]]周辺の位置関係図。破線は江戸時代初期ごろの旧河道、緑線・緑字は開削・付替後の新河道。橙線・橙字は主要街道、赤字は主要な地名など。]] 尾張国津島社（後の津島神社）は[[式外社]]で正確な成立時期は不明である。伝承では[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]）の創建とされる。津島神社所蔵の天文9年（[[1540年]]書写の『牛頭天王講式』によると、日本に渡った牛頭天王は[[孝霊天皇]]の代に[[対馬]]から西国に渡来し、欽明天皇の代に[[四神相応]]の霊地として尾張国海部郡門真荘津島に[[垂迹]]したという{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |author=松山 由布子 |title=津島神社所蔵『牛頭天王講式』と奥三河伝来『牛頭天王五段式』の関わりについて |journal=愛知県史研究 |volume=21 |issue= |publisher=愛知県 |year=2017 |pages= 57-72}}。しかし、先述のように[[延喜式神名帳]]には記載されていない式外社で国史にも現れない。年代が明確な史料では、名古屋七寺所蔵の一切経のうち[[承安 (日本)|承安]]5年（[[1175年]]）の正月から七月までに書写された『大般若経』巻末の摺写勧請文に津島社の記載がある。 16世紀頃には津島衆と呼ばれる四家七党の同族の社家組織によって運営され、織田信長、[[豊臣秀吉]]、[[徳川家康]]などの援助を受けた。その後、尾張藩の庇護を受けて氷室家を神主とする社家組織が確立された。正保4年（[[1647年]]）4月には尾張藩主[[徳川義直]]から津島向島1293石8升9合の[[朱印状]]を受け、この社領は寛文5年（[[1665年]]）に将軍の[[徳川家綱]]により朱印地として認められた。 津島社の信仰は社家やその手代が御師（津島御師）として各地で布教活動を行い、その信仰を受け入れた村人は旦那、旦那が所在する村は旦那場と称された。 [[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、建物・祭事などにおけるあらゆる[[仏教]]的な要素は廃され、祭神を建速須佐之男命とし、社名から牛頭天王の名を外して津島神社としたが、いまも「津島の天王さま」と呼ばれ、全国約3000社の天王信仰の総本社である[https://tsushimajinja.or.jp/ 津島神社HP「由緒」] 2022年6月27日閲覧。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に県社に列格し、[[大正]]15年（[[1926年]]）に国幣小社に昇格した。 神社のすぐそばに[[天王川公園]]がある。公園の中央には楕円形の「丸池」があるが、これはかつて津島神社と門前町の間を流れた[[天王川 (津島市)|天王川]]の名残である。かつての天王川は[[木曽川]]左派川末端部の大河川であり、津島神社前に架けられた「天王橋」は長大な橋であったことが[[宗長]]の『宗長手記』に記されている。江戸時代となり木曽川に[[御囲堤]]が築かれると、天王川と下流の[[佐屋川]]は河床上昇により水害が増加した。その対策として[[日光川]]が開削されたことで天王川は津島神社付近で築留めされたため入り江状となり、[[明治]]に行われた[[木曽三川分流工事]]で佐屋川も廃川となったため楕円形の池が残された。牛頭天王は排斥されたが、今も「丸池」で行われる神社の祭りは[[尾張津島天王祭]]として「天王」の名が残っている。 [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）[[11月23日]]、神社近くの民家から出火、折からの強風で神社本殿の[[檜皮葺]]屋根に飛び火した。懸命な消火活動が行われたが、屋根部分を全焼する被害重文 津島神社の屋根焼く『朝日新聞』1970年（昭和45年）11月24日 12版 22面。 == 主な年中行事 == [[ファイル:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|尾張津島天王祭]] 津島神社の祭事は年間100を超える。 * 七草祭（旧正月7日間） * 祈年祭（3月17日） * 例祭（6月17日） * {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[津島神社]] to [[Tsushima Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|愛知県津島市にある津島神社|その他の津島神社|津島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{神社 | 名称=津島神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tsushima-jinja_Shrine_Haiden_(1),_Shimmei-cho_Tsushima_City_2023.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿
（2023年（令和5年）4月）
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|10|41.6|N|136|43|7.1|E}}}} | 所在地=[[愛知県]][[津島市]]神明町1 | ISO = JP-23 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 10|緯度秒 = 41.65 | 経度度 =136|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 7.13 | 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] | 創建=[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]） | 社格=旧[[国幣小社]]
[[別表神社]] | 本殿=[[尾張造]] | 例祭=[[6月15日]] | 神事=津島天王祭（7月第4土曜日） | 地図=Japan Aichi }} '''津島神社'''（つしまじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[神社]]。京都・[[八坂神社]]とともに牛頭天王信仰の二大社として知られる{{Cite web|和書|title=第四章 津島神社の「花の撓」|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/minzoku/mukei_kiroku/pdf/r1392266_08.pdf |website=文化庁 |access-date=2023-12-30}}。 == 概要 == [[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]包括の[[別表神社]]。 [[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、大穴牟遅命（[[大国主]]）を相殿に祀る。伝承では[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]）の創建とされる{{Cite web|和書|title=第2章 維持及び向上すべき歴史的風致|url=https://www.city.tsushima.lg.jp/shisei/machidukuri/rekisitekihuuti/tsushimashirekimachi.files/2syo.pdf |website=津島市 |access-date=2023-12-30}}。実際の創建時期ははっきりしないが、12世紀の文献に現れ、鎌倉時代から室町時代にかけての鉄灯籠や梵鐘の銘には社名が刻まれている。近世以前は[[牛頭天王]]を祭神として祀る神社として知られた{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.14.0_15 |author=松下 孜 |title=天王信仰と津島御師の活動 御立符と檀那場をめぐって |journal=愛知県史研究 |volume=14 |issue= |publisher=愛知県 |year=2010 |pages= 15-37}}。 近世には織田氏や豊臣氏の庇護を受けて社殿造営等の寄進を受けた。さらに尾張藩からも神領所有の特権を得るとともに、津島神社の川祭りで寄進米などを受ける特権が与えられた。 当社は[[東海地方]]を中心に全国に約3千社ある津島神社・天王社の総本社であり、その信仰を[[津島信仰]]という。津島神社が諸国に勢力を拡大した背景には、御札を配って祈祷を行う津島御師の活動が大きく、織田信長は御師の布教活動を保護し、尾張藩もこの保護政策を継承して他領での布教活動に許可を与えた。 明治の[[神仏分離令]]までは「津島牛頭天王社」とも称された。 == 歴史 == [[File:Old tenno river map.jpg|thumb|250px|right|江戸時代末ごろの[[天王川 (津島市)|天王川]]周辺の位置関係図。破線は江戸時代初期ごろの旧河道、緑線・緑字は開削・付替後の新河道。橙線・橙字は主要街道、赤字は主要な地名など。]] 尾張国津島社（後の津島神社）は[[式外社]]で正確な成立時期は不明である。伝承では[[欽明天皇]]元年（[[540年]]）の創建とされる。津島神社所蔵の天文9年（[[1540年]]書写の『牛頭天王講式』によると、日本に渡った牛頭天王は[[孝霊天皇]]の代に[[対馬]]から西国に渡来し、欽明天皇の代に[[四神相応]]の霊地として尾張国海部郡門真荘津島に[[垂迹]]したという{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://doi.org/10.24707/aichikenshikenkyu.21.0_57 |author=松山 由布子 |title=津島神社所蔵『牛頭天王講式』と奥三河伝来『牛頭天王五段式』の関わりについて |journal=愛知県史研究 |volume=21 |issue= |publisher=愛知県 |year=2017 |pages= 57-72}}。しかし、先述のように[[延喜式神名帳]]には記載されていない式外社で国史にも現れない。年代が明確な史料では、名古屋七寺所蔵の一切経のうち[[承安 (日本)|承安]]5年（[[1175年]]）の正月から七月までに書写された『大般若経』巻末の摺写勧請文に津島社の記載がある。 16世紀頃には津島衆と呼ばれる四家七党の同族の社家組織によって運営され、織田信長、[[豊臣秀吉]]、[[徳川家康]]などの援助を受けた。その後、尾張藩の庇護を受けて氷室家を神主とする社家組織が確立された。正保4年（[[1647年]]）4月には尾張藩主[[徳川義直]]から津島向島1293石8升9合の[[朱印状]]を受け、この社領は寛文5年（[[1665年]]）に将軍の[[徳川家綱]]により朱印地として認められた。 津島社の信仰は社家やその手代が御師（津島御師）として各地で布教活動を行い、その信仰を受け入れた村人は旦那、旦那が所在する村は旦那場と称された。 [[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、建物・祭事などにおけるあらゆる[[仏教]]的な要素は廃され、祭神を建速須佐之男命とし、社名から牛頭天王の名を外して津島神社としたが、いまも「津島の天王さま」と呼ばれ、全国約3000社の天王信仰の総本社である[https://tsushimajinja.or.jp/ 津島神社HP「由緒」] 2022年6月27日閲覧。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に県社に列格し、[[大正]]15年（[[1926年]]）に国幣小社に昇格した。 神社のすぐそばに[[天王川公園]]がある。公園の中央には楕円形の「丸池」があるが、これはかつて津島神社と門前町の間を流れた[[天王川 (津島市)|天王川]]の名残である。かつての天王川は[[木曽川]]左派川末端部の大河川であり、津島神社前に架けられた「天王橋」は長大な橋であったことが[[宗長]]の『宗長手記』に記されている。江戸時代となり木曽川に[[御囲堤]]が築かれると、天王川と下流の[[佐屋川]]は河床上昇により水害が増加した。その対策として[[日光川]]が開削されたことで天王川は津島神社付近で築留めされたため入り江状となり、[[明治]]に行われた[[木曽三川分流工事]]で佐屋川も廃川となったため楕円形の池が残された。牛頭天王は排斥されたが、今も「丸池」で行われる神社の祭りは[[尾張津島天王祭]]として「天王」の名が残っている。 [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）[[11月23日]]、神社近くの民家から出火、折からの強風で神社本殿の[[檜皮葺]]屋根に飛び火した。懸命な消火活動が行われたが、屋根部分を全焼する被害重文 津島神社の屋根焼く『朝日新聞』1970年（昭和45年）11月24日 12版 22面。 == 主な年中行事 == {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsushima, Aichi]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Aichi prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:津島市の建築物]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons Category|Tsushima-jinja (Tsushima, Aichi)|Tsushima Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Gion cult}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|津島神社|version=101876956|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Owari-zukuri]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[Category:Tsushima Shrines]] [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]][[sn:Tsushima Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tsushima Shrine]] [[en:Tsushima Shrine]] [[fr:Tsushima-jinja]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E5%B3%B6%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:Natural monuments of Aichi prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:津島市の建築物]] [[Category:津島市の歴史]]

are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons revision.comment: Bot: update category → Buildings in Tsushima, Aichi
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tsushima Shrine
津島神社 | image = Tsushimajinja4.JPG | caption = The [[Haiden (Shinto)|''haiden'']] of Tsushima Shrine | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|42|N|136|43|07|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Tsushima Shrine | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]/[[Gion|Gozutennō]] | founded_by = | established = 540 AD | date_destroyed = | location = Tsushima, Aichi | website = {{URL|http://tsushimajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tsushima Shrine'''|津島神社|Tsushima Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]], [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is the head shrine of a nationwide [[Shinto shrine#Shrine networks|shrine network]] of shrines dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tsushima Cult|津島信仰|''Tsushima Shinkō''}}, Centered primarily in the [[Tōkai region]], this network has approximately 3,000 shrines and is the tenth-largest network in the country. The main ''[[kami]]'' of this faith are {{nihongo|[[Gozu Tennō]]|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lit. ox-headed heaven king}}, the god of pestilences, and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], two deities that have been conflated together.{{EOS|Gozu Tennō|192|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} For this reason, like other shrines of the network it is also called {{nihongo|Tsushima Gozutennō-sha|津島牛頭天王社|lit. Tsushima Gozutennō Shrine}}.{{EOS|Gion/Tsushima Shinkō|1075|Yonei, Teruyoshi|2-5-2011}} See [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] for more info. ==History== Shrine legend, unsupported by any historical documentation, claims that the shrine was founded in [[Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima]] by the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōrei]] (343-215 BCE) to worship Gozutennō's ''[[Mitama|aramitama]]'' (its violent side), which remained at [[Izumo-taisha]], and it's ''[[Mitama|nigemitama]] (calm aspect)'' which came to Japan from the [[Korean peninsula]] after stopping in [[Tsushima Island]], between Korea and Japan. The shrine relocated to its current location in [[Owari Province]] in 540 CE. This may explain the relationship between the two Tsushimas suggested by the common name. The shrine appears in historical records from the time of [[Emperor Saga]] (786-846 CE), during whose Rank it was awarded the status of First Court Rank, indicating that it was of considerable importance and antiquity by that time. It was awarded the title of ''Tennō-sha'' by [[Emperor Ichijō]] (980-1011 CE); however, for unknown reasons, it is not mentioned at all in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records completed in 927 CE, nor in the official records of the province. In the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Oda clan]] built Shobata Castle in the vicinity of the shrine, and the [[Mon (emblem)|family crest]] of the Oda clan is the same emblem as that used by the Tsushima Shrine, indicating a close connection. The shrine was subsequently repaired by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and it received official status and patronage by the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] of [[Owari Domain]] under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] in the [[Edo period]]. With the establishment of [[State Shinto]] in the [[Meiji period]], Tsushima Shine was initially Ranked as a prefectural shrine in [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], and its status was increased to that of a ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]'' (国幣小社), or National Shrine, 3rd Rank, in 1926. In 1920, the ''[[Honden]]'' of the shrine, which was built in 1605 under the patronage of [[Matsudaira Tadayoshi]], was designated an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The building is built in the [[Shinto architecture#Owari-zukuri|owari-zukuri]] style, of which few examples remain.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174472 |title=津島神社本殿 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The ''[[Rōmon]]'' gate, built in 1591, was also designated an Important Cultural Property in 1954.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/189073 |title=津島神社楼門 |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }} The shrine holds a festival called {{nihongo|Tsushima Matsuri|津島祭り}} in the sixth month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] (July in the [[Gregorian calendar]]) during which boats called {{nihongo|''danjiri''|車楽}} are floated on the Tennō River, and reeds are released into the water. ==Gallery== File:Tsushimajinja3.JPG|Rōmon File:Tsushimatennosai1.JPG|[[Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennōsai(eve)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{commons
